front: What is the role of endothelin-1 in pulmonary hypertension?
back: Endothelin-1 (ET-1, 21 aa) is the most potent vasoconstrictor known. It acts on ETA (constriction, proliferation) and ETB (vasodilation, clearance) receptors. In pulmonary hypertension, ET-1 is overexpressed, causing vascular remodeling and elevated pulmonary pressures.
